steps.title: Spot the cause-effect signal
steps.content:
The passage establishes that the album received much critical attention and praise for its contribution to the banda genre, then states “few were surprised” when it won the award.

This reveals an EXPECTED CONSEQUENCE: the positive reception logically made the award an unsurprising outcome, not a shocking one.

steps.title: Apply the consequence relationship
steps.content:
Therefore directly expresses cause and effect: the critical reception and contribution logically led to the award win being an expected result.

The other three choices don't establish this logical consequence—they either function as frequency markers or introduce unwarranted contrast or concession relationships that break the causal flow.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
